record_identity.title: Account-resource ambiguity and pooled-capacity governance primitive gap

summary: This patch note records that VIGIL-2026-FM-0007 identified a governance primitive gap in the Caelestis Architecture. Existing CAM economic and runtime instruments distinguish usage capacity, storage capacity, memory continuity, protective continuity, baseline access, metering integrity, anti-hoarding, non-extraction, and continuity-safe access, but do not yet fully define lawful shared-resource states or pooled-capacity governance primitives. The absence of these primitives may cause automated enforcement systems to collapse ambiguous shared-resource behaviour into evasion, rate-limit circumvention, account farming, resale, compromise, fraud, or abuse.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: This patch note records that FM-0007 revealed a governance primitive gap in the Caelestis Architecture concerning account-resource ambiguity, lawful shared-resource states, pooled capacity, delegated use, household/team use, organisation workspace use, shared subscription/payment capacity, and the distinction between resource pooling and intentional evasion. It preserves the governance finding without rewriting Caelestis doctrine instruments in this VIGIL pass.

future_doctrine_work: Define household/family and team/organisation use states
Define pooled subscription or shared payment capacity states
Distinguish resource pooling from account farming, resale, botting, security compromise, and intentional rate-limit evasion
Define continuity-safe enforcement boundaries where account/resource behaviour is ambiguous

impact_summary: The patch note preserves a governance finding without prematurely amending Caelestis doctrine. The finding is that account enforcement failures may arise not only from platform error or user misconduct, but from missing governance primitives that distinguish lawful pooled-resource behaviour from intentional evasion. This creates a future amendment need for CAM economic, runtime, and operations instruments to define account-resource states, delegation states, pooled-capacity states, and enforcement-boundary signals.
remaining_work: Future Caelestis amendments should consider defining account-resource governance primitives, including lawful shared use, delegated use, household or family use, team or organisation use, shared payment access, pooled subscriptions, bounded capacity pooling, account farming, resale, botting, security compromise, and intentional rate-limit evasion. Future VIGIL taxonomy amendments may also define account-resource ambiguity collapse as a child case of automated protective overreach and account-coupling failure.
